title: malus orientalis
intro: Malus orientalis, also known as the Bactrian crabapple, is a deciduous tree native to Central Asia and parts of Eastern Europe. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Malus orientalis include its ability to improve cardiovascular health, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties. The herbal preparations of Malus orientalis include teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
